verification.note: AGENTS.md: whole-file interchange artifact; broadly read by multiple agents, never a source of truth (ADR-001). .agents/skills/: verified cross-tool skill discovery path — Codex reads $CWD/.agents/skills, $CWD/../.agents/skills, $REPO_ROOT/.agents/skills, $HOME/.agents/skills, /etc/codex/skills (learn.chatgpt.com/docs/build-skills); Gemini CLI reads .agents/skills/ and prioritizes it over its own native .gemini/skills/ (geminicli.com/docs/cli/skills/). Neither reads .claude/skills/.

limitations: AGENTS.md itself is whole-file only: no path scoping, no policy enforcement.

.agents/skills/ emits the STRICTLY PORTABLE Agent Skills frontmatter only (name, description, license, compatibility, metadata, allowed-tools) — Claude-only extensions (when_to_use, disable-model-invocation, paths) are never carried here.
Duplicate skill names across .agents/skills/ scope tiers are NOT merged by Codex — both remain selectable, so avoid relying on override-by-name across tiers.
